entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
Students read about the story of cartography from ancient Mesopotamia up through the discovery of Antarctica in the late 1800s and draw maps along the way. The course finishes with a term of historical geography where students follow the steps of Canadian explorers from the 19th century and learn about Canadian provinces through map work.

Geography: Grade 7 (Canada)
The book used in Term 3—Exploring the Frozen North by Pierre Berton—is a compendium of several shorter books written for younger readers by Berton on the theme of Arctic exploration. One such book is assigned for the course: Trapped in the Arctic. This collection, however, is the most cost-effective and accessible way to get the book. The other selections would make interesting free reads or read-alouds, but because there is some sensitive content in the other books, parents may prefer to preread.

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content: The first two terms are the same every year and shared between both US and Canadian courses. Term 3 brings in resources from the historical time period and is specific to Canadian students.

Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.
